official_name: National Institute of Standards and Technology
notes: NIST reference documents — IR (Internal/Interagency Reports), TN (Technical Notes), CSWP (Cybersecurity White Papers), CSRC glossary content, and AI RMF crosswalks. This is the reference type entry; NIST's normative control publications (SP 800-53, CSF, etc.) live under control type.

data.description: Research and technical reports published by NIST staff; less formal than Special Publications but authoritative.
Cybersecurity white papers from NIST CSRC.
Authoritative glossary of NIST cybersecurity terms aggregated from NIST publications.
NIST-published crosswalks between AI RMF 1.0 and other AI governance frameworks (ISO/IEC 23894, OECD AI Principles, EU AI Act, Blueprint for an AI Bill of Rights).

NIST Special Publication 800-series documents that are guidance/informative rather than control catalogs. Examples include SP 800-100 (Information Security Handbook), SP 800-115 (Technical Guide to Security Testing), SP 800-122 (PII Confidentiality), SP 800-145 (Cloud Definition), SP 800-160 v2 (Cyber-Resiliency Considerations). Catalog-style SP 800-series documents (800-53, 800-171, etc.) are registered under control/gov/nist; process-style ones (800-30, 800-37, 800-39, 800-61) are registered under methodology/gov/nist.
data.notes: Many SP 800-numbered documents have content shaped as guidance prose rather than per-control catalogs. Resolution from a numeric source name (e.g., "800-100") defers to the document's metadata `secid` field for type disambiguation.

data.description: NCCoE (National Cybersecurity Center of Excellence) practice guides demonstrating reference implementations of cybersecurity capabilities. Informative reference documents, not control catalogs.

data.official_name: NIST FIPS algorithm and categorization specs
data.description: FIPS publications that specify cryptographic algorithms (FIPS 197 AES, FIPS 180 SHA, FIPS 186 DSS, FIPS 198 HMAC, FIPS 201 PIV, FIPS 202 SHA-3, FIPS 203/204/205 post-quantum) or that defer requirement content to external standards (FIPS 140-2 withdrawn, FIPS 140-3 deferring to ISO/IEC 19790). Categorization frameworks with structurable catalogs (FIPS 199, FIPS 200) are registered under control/gov/nist.
data.notes: FIPS 140-2 and 140-3 land here rather than under control because their structurable content is too thin to register as a control catalog (140-3 defers to ISO/IEC 19790:2012(E) which is not redistributable; 140-2 was withdrawn in 2022).

data.description: NIST Artificial Intelligence series publications such as AI 100-4. Complements the AI RMF (AI 100-1) and AI 600-1 GenAI Profile (both registered under control/gov/nist when structured catalog-like).
